title: Subjunctive in Adverbial Clauses
description: Choose indicative or subjunctive after time, concession and purpose conjunctions: cuando, en cuanto, hasta que, aunque, antes de que, a menos que, para que.

cards.back: When you arrive, call me. (future → subjunctive)
cards.notes: A time clause about the **future/unknown** takes the subjunctive: *cuando **llegues***. Habitual or past → indicative: *Cuando **llego**, siempre…*
*En cuanto **termine**, te aviso.* — As soon as I finish, I'll let you know (future → subjunctive).
*Espera hasta que **vuelva**.* — Wait until I come back (future → subjunctive).

cards.back: even if he comes (subj.) vs. although he is coming (ind.)
cards.notes: **aunque + subjunctive** = even if (hypothetical); **aunque + indicative** = although (a known fact).
*Antes de que **salgas**, cierra la puerta.* — *antes de que* **always** takes the subjunctive.
*No iré a menos que me **invites**.* — I won't go unless you invite me.
Purpose with a change of subject → subjunctive: *Te lo explico **para que** lo **entiendas**.*

steps.title: Time clauses: future = subjunctive
steps.body:
# Indicative or subjunctive?

Many conjunctions can take **either** mood. The rule for **time** clauses:

- Refers to the **future / something not yet real** → **subjunctive**
- Refers to the **habitual or the past (a fact)** → **indicative**

| Conjunction | Future (subj.) | Habitual/past (ind.) |
|---|---|---|
| cuando | Cuando **llegues**, llámame. | Cuando **llego**, como. |
| en cuanto | En cuanto **termine**… | En cuanto **termino**… |
| hasta que | Espera hasta que **vuelva**. | Esperé hasta que **volvió**. |

English hides this — *When you arrive* uses the present, but Spanish needs the **subjunctive** for the still-unreal future.

steps.title: Concession and the always-subjunctive group
steps.body:
# aunque and the fixed triggers

## aunque — even if vs. although

- **aunque + subjunctive** = *even if* (hypothetical): *Aunque **llueva**, saldré.* — Even if it rains, I'll go out.
- **aunque + indicative** = *although* (a fact): *Aunque **llueve**, salgo.* — Although it's raining, I go out.

## Always subjunctive

These conjunctions **always** take the subjunctive (the action is unreal, anticipated or purpose-driven):

- **antes de que** — before: *antes de que **salgas***
- **a menos que** — unless: *a menos que me **invites***
- **para que** — so that: *para que lo **entiendas***
- **sin que** — without: *sin que lo **sepa***

Learn this group as a fixed list.

exercise.prompt: Match each Spanish conjunction with its English meaning.
Fill in the subjunctive of llegar (tú): "When you arrive, call me."
exercise.hint: Future reference after cuando → present subjunctive.

exercise.prompt: Fill in the subjunctive of salir (tú): "Before you leave, close the door."
exercise.sentence: Antes de que ___, cierra la puerta.
exercise.hint: antes de que always takes the subjunctive; salir (yo salgo) → salga-stem.

exercise.prompt: Translate: *Even if it rains, I will go out.* (aunque + subjunctive of llover; salir → future)
Arrange: *I'll explain it to you so that you understand it.*
